Title: Two B-box proteins, PavBBX6/9, positively regulate light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in sweet cherry

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins at the 3-O position to form stable anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration and nutritional antioxidant pigment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PavUFGT expression is associated with and supports increased anthocyanin accumulation under light; it is activated by PavBBX6/9.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under light/ABA treatments; transient expression/silencing of upstream regulators PavBBX6/9 followed by PavUFGT expression analysis; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in fruit by light; increased with ABA treatment in light; reduced by NDGA treatment; higher in PavBBX6/9-overexpressing calli and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in fruit by light; increased with ABA treatment in light; reduced by NDGA treatment; higher in PavBBX6/9-overexpressing calli and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bag removal/light exposure; exogenous ABA; NDGA; dark vs light
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PavUFGT promoter; CaMV 35S used for upstream regulator overexpression assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target/marker for engineering higher anthocyanin fruit coloration, especially together with upstream activators such as PavBBX6/9 under light-responsive production contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in the paper as a key structural gene for anthocyanin biosynthesis in bicolored sweet cherry.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavUFGT is a key structural gene for anthocyanin biosynthesis in bicolored sweet cherry. 2. Its expression is strongly induced by light and enhanced by ABA under light conditions. 3. PavBBX6 and PavBBX9 directly bind the G-box in the PavUFGT promoter and activate its transcription, thereby promoting anthocyanin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid cleavage branch leading to ABA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a direct precursor in ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ripening-related hormone that promotes anthocyanin accumulation in sweet cherry fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA biosynthesis and thereby supports anthocyanin accumulation; expression is activated by PavBBX6/9.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under light/ABA treatments; overexpression in calli and transient fruit assays via upstream PavBBX6/9; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by light in fruit; increased by exogenous ABA; elevated in PavBBX6/9-overexpressing calli and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by light in fruit; increased by exogenous ABA; elevated in PavBBX6/9-overexpressing calli and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bag removal/light exposure; exogenous ABA; NDGA; dark vs light
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PavNCED1 promoter; CaMV 35S used for upstream regulator overexpression assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate leverage point for boosting ABA-associated coloration, but should be used cautiously due to broad ABA effects; suitable for stacking with anthocyanin pathway regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible pleiotropic ABA-related effects are plausible but not directly reported in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as an important ABA biosynthesis gene in sweet cherry that promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavNCED1 is a key ABA biosynthetic gene induced by light in sweet cherry fruit. 2. Increased ABA promotes anthocyanin accumulation under light. 3. PavBBX6 and PavBBX9 directly bind the G-box in the PavNCED1 promoter and activate its transcription, linking light signaling to ABA production.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box zinc finger protein; BBX family; BBX21-like
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel/pericarp and transformed calli under light
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanin contributes to red fruit coloration and antioxidant nutritional quality; ABA promotes ripening-associated coloration
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PavUFGT and PavNCED1 transcription by direct promoter binding to G-box elements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates light-induced anthocyanin accumulation and ABA biosynthesis; overexpression increases both metabolites, while silencing reduces anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive light- and ABA-responsive regulator that links light signaling to both PavUFGT-mediated anthocyanin synthesis and PavNCED1-mediated ABA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; cell-free degradation assay; Arabidopsis ectopic overexpression; stable overexpression in sweet cherry calli; transient overexpression and RNAi silencing in fruit peel; anthocyanin/ABA metabolite profiling; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No direct complex experimentally shown in this paper; discussion proposes possible COP1-mediated regulation by analogy
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced by light and by light+ABA; inhibited by light+NDGA; protein degraded faster in dark and stabilized by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced by light and by light+ABA; inhibited by light+NDGA; overexpression in calli/fruit promotes target genes under light
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; ABA; NDGA-sensitive ABA biosynthetic status
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bag removal/light exposure; exogenous ABA; NDGA; dark vs light
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop with ABA: PavBBX6 activates PavNCED1 to raise ABA, and ABA promotes PavBBX6 expression/protein stability
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression; native PavUFGT and PavNCED1 promoters used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for improving red coloration and anthocyanin content in sweet cherry through overexpression or promoter engineering under light-responsive contexts; stacking with structural genes or ABA pathway targets may further enhance effect.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct growth/yield penalty reported in cherry; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis affects photomorphogenesis (shorter hypocotyls), indicating broader light-development effects are possible.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PavBBX6 has somewhat weaker effect than PavBBX9 in some assays; functional redundancy with PavBBX9 is suggested.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavBBX6 is induced by light and ABA, and its protein is less stable in darkness but stabilized by ABA. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis, sweet cherry calli, and fruit increases anthocyanin and ABA accumulation, while silencing suppresses anthocyanin. 3. PavBBX6 directly binds G-box elements in PavUFGT and PavNCED1 promoters and activates their transcription, linking light and ABA signaling to fruit coloration.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box zinc finger protein; BBX family; BBX22-like
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel/pericarp and transformed calli under light
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanin contributes to red fruit coloration and antioxidant nutritional quality; ABA promotes ripening-associated coloration
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PavUFGT and PavNCED1 transcription by direct promoter binding to G-box elements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates light-induced anthocyanin accumulation and ABA biosynthesis; overexpression increases both metabolites, and silencing has strong suppressive effects, especially on PavNCED1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator linking light signaling to anthocyanin and ABA production; appears to contribute more strongly than PavBBX6 in this system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; cell-free degradation assay; Arabidopsis ectopic overexpression; stable overexpression in sweet cherry calli; transient overexpression and RNAi silencing in fruit peel; anthocyanin/ABA metabolite profiling; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No direct complex experimentally shown in this paper; discussion proposes possible COP1-mediated regulation by analogy
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced by light and by light+ABA; inhibited by light+NDGA; protein degraded faster in dark and stabilized by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced by light and by light+ABA; overexpression in calli/fruit strongly promotes target genes under light
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; ABA; NDGA-sensitive ABA biosynthetic status
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bag removal/light exposure; exogenous ABA; NDGA; dark vs light
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop with ABA: PavBBX9 activates PavNCED1 to increase ABA, and ABA promotes PavBBX9 expression/protein stability
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression; native PavUFGT and PavNCED1 promoters used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for enhancing sweet cherry peel coloration and anthocyanin content under light; may be especially effective due to stronger phenotype than PavBBX6.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct growth/yield penalty reported in cherry; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis shortens hypocotyls, suggesting possible broader developmental effects.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper indicates PavBBX9 may play a greater role than PavBBX6 and that the two genes may act redundantly in ABA-mediated anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavBBX9 is induced by light and ABA, and its protein is destabilized in darkness but stabilized by ABA. 2. Overexpression in Arabidopsis, sweet cherry calli, and fruit elevates anthocyanin and ABA, whereas silencing reduces anthocyanin and lowers PavNCED1 expression more clearly than PavBBX6 silencing. 3. PavBBX9 directly binds G-box elements in PavUFGT and PavNCED1 promoters and activates their transcription, making it a strong positive regulator of light-induced fruit coloration.
